title: Verily Internship
subtitle: Software Engineering Intern working on a retinal camera
summary: I found this internship at late March when I almost lost hope in job searching, and it started in May, so everything felt lucky. It was my first time ever been to California and my first time ever do a "real" summer internship.

image.alt: I happened to visit Google's Bayview campus when it first opened

blocks.text: Back then, Verily Life Sciences was still a subsidiary of Alphabet and we still had access to Google buildings. So the internship feels both like working in a large company in Google and working in a startup where everything is shifty.

It was my first time in California, but I did not know how to drive. So I did not get a lot chances to explore the bay area but still managed to check out the Golden Gate Bridge, the California Coast and even made several short trips to visit my friends in Boston and Seattle.

Our team was working on a retinal camera project, which was not launched back then but were to be canceled in 2025. I worked on building a visualization tool to help the development of the retinal imaging algorithms. But work was the least thing I can remember now when I think of this summer.
Many internship experiences are talked here:

items: [找实习和找工作——Cornell ECE MENG 就读体验 - 3](https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/673614414)
